job_summary: 4,500 sf commercial office tenant improvement on the 3rd floor in Los Angeles: new demising partitions, office buildout, ceiling and lighting changes, sprinkler head relocation, electrical/data work, and accessible path of travel upgrades. This triggers a commercial Building Permit for the TI/alteration, plus separate trade permits for electrical and fire sprinkler work. Because this is a commercial interior alteration with accessibility scope, expect plan check and coordination with LADBS; if the project affects the public right-of-way or exterior approach, LA Bureau of Engineering review may also be needed.

permits_required.permit_type: Building Permit — Commercial Tenant Improvement / Interior Alteration
permits_required.notes: Required for the new demising partitions, office buildout, ceiling modifications, and accessible path of travel work. Commercial TI work in LA is plan-check driven and typically requires architectural sheets, code analysis, and accessibility details.

permits_required.permit_type: Electrical Permit — Commercial Interior Wiring / Lighting / Data
permits_required.notes: Required for new lighting changes, branch circuit work, and any new electrical/data pathways that involve power, raceways, devices, or panel modifications.

permits_required.permit_type: Fire Protection Permit — Fire Sprinkler Head Relocation
permits_required.notes: Required because sprinkler heads are being relocated. This is separate from the building permit and usually requires fire sprinkler shop drawings and coordination with the fire protection contractor.

permits_required_logic.permit_type: Building Permit — Commercial Tenant Improvement / Interior Alteration
permits_required_logic.included_because: The scope includes new demising partitions, office buildout, ceiling changes, and accessible path of travel improvements, all of which are commercial interior alterations that require building plan check.
permits_required_logic.scope_trigger: Commercial tenant improvement with partition layout changes and accessibility upgrades

permits_required_logic.permit_type: Electrical Permit — Commercial Interior Wiring / Lighting / Data
permits_required_logic.included_because: The scope explicitly includes electrical/data work and lighting changes, which triggers an electrical permit for new or modified circuits, devices, lighting controls, and related wiring.
permits_required_logic.scope_trigger: Electrical and lighting changes in tenant improvement scope

permits_required_logic.permit_type: Fire Protection Permit — Fire Sprinkler Head Relocation
permits_required_logic.included_because: Relocating sprinkler heads is fire protection work and requires a separate fire sprinkler permit and inspection, not just a building permit.

applying_office: Los Angeles Department of Building and Safety (LADBS)
apply_address: 201 N. Figueroa Street, Los Angeles, CA 90012
fee_range: Fee Estimate: **$21,000-$30,500+** (structured floor — see breakdown). Components: ~$11,000 base permit + plan review (Los Angeles, CA commercial office TI floor) × 1.5× jurisdiction multiplier + $2,000 demising-wall adder + $4,000 fire-sprinkler-modify adder + $3,000 ada-path-of-travel adder. **Verify against current fee schedule before quoting.**

approval_timeline.simple: Over-the-counter intake is possible for straightforward trade permits, but this commercial TI will usually go to plan check; expect 3-8 weeks for review and corrections.
approval_timeline.complex: 8-16+ weeks if accessibility, fire protection, structural, or multiple correction cycles are involved

inspections.description: Inspector checks partition framing layout, fireblocking/draftstopping where required, rated wall assemblies at tenant separations, door hardware/egress clearances, and that work matches approved plans before drywall closes.
inspections.timing: Before insulation, drywall, or ceiling closure

inspections.description: Inspector verifies branch-circuit routing, box fill, conductor sizing, grounding/bonding, lighting rough-in, emergency/egress lighting provisions if shown on plans, and proper support of raceways and devices.
inspections.timing: Before walls and ceilings are covered

inspections.stage: Fire Sprinkler Rough-In / Hydrostatic or Head Relocation Inspection
inspections.description: Inspector checks sprinkler head relocation locations, spacing, obstruction clearances, pipe support, head type/temperature rating, and that the revised layout matches the approved fire protection submittal.
inspections.timing: Before ceiling closure and before system is returned to service

inspections.description: Inspector verifies accessible path of travel, door clear widths, maneuvering clearances, hardware, signage, restroom accessibility if altered, slopes/thresholds, and that the completed TI matches approved accessibility details.

inspection_booking: Schedule inspections through the LADBS online permit/inspection system at https://www.ladbs.org/services/apply-for-a-permit or by calling (213) 482-0000. For City of Los Angeles inspections, use 311 inside the city or the automated inspection line at (888) 524-2845 / (888) LA4-BUILD. Minimum 24 hours advance notice is the safe rule for scheduling; book earlier for commercial work because multiple trades may need separate inspections.
license_required: Licensed California general contractor or the appropriate licensed trade contractor pulls the permit on the tenant improvement. For the electrical scope, a C-10 contractor pulls the electrical permit; for sprinkler work, the fire protection contractor pulls the sprinkler permit; the contractor's license number must appear on the application. The permit is still required even when a licensed contractor submits it.
city_contractor_registration: Los Angeles City requires a CSLB license and an LA City Business Tax Registration Certificate (BTRC) for contractors submitting permits. Verify the BTRC is current before filing; an expired registration can block permit issuance at the counter or in the portal.
what_to_bring: Completed LADBS permit application and project address/tenant suite number
Architectural plan set showing demolition, new partitions, reflected ceiling plan, life safety/egress, and accessibility path of travel details
Electrical sheets with lighting layout, panel schedule changes, and data/power routing
Fire sprinkler shop drawings or sprinkler contractor submittal for head relocation
Contractor CSLB license number and LA City Business Tax Registration Certificate
Title 24 energy compliance documents and any required accessibility calculations
common_mistakes: Submitting the TI without the LA City Business Tax Registration Certificate — LADBS can reject or stall the application even if the CSLB license is valid
Forgetting that sprinkler head relocation is a separate fire protection permit and inspection, not just a building note on the plans
Leaving out Title 24 energy documentation for lighting changes and any altered controls
Missing accessible path of travel details on the plan set — this can trigger correction cycles and delay issuance
Assuming data cabling is exempt when the scope also includes power, lighting, or device changes
Starting demolition before the permit is issued and posted — LADBS can issue a stop-work order
pro_tips: Have the LA City BTRC and CSLB license info ready before opening the portal; that is one of the fastest ways to avoid a rejected submittal
If the suite is near the street or work touches the sidewalk/curb/approach, check whether LA Bureau of Engineering review is needed in parallel with LADBS
For commercial TI, submit a clean code summary sheet up front: occupancy, occupant load, egress, accessibility, and fire/life safety. It cuts correction cycles
Bring the sprinkler contractor into the permit process early; head relocation often becomes the schedule bottleneck because the fire protection submittal must match the ceiling plan
If lighting is being redesigned, coordinate Title 24 compliance before plan check so you do not get hit with a correction for controls, wattage, or occupancy sensor requirements

code_citation.text: 105.1 Required. Any owner or authorized agent who intends to construct, enlarge, alter, repair, move, demolish or change the occupancy of a building or structure shall first make application to the building official...

companion_permits.reason: May be required if: HVAC, duct, exhaust, or ventilation scope is added to the TI.
companion_permits.required_if: HVAC, duct, exhaust, or ventilation scope is added to the TI
companion_permits.requirement_label: May be required based on scope

companion_permits.permit_type: Plumbing Permit — Commercial Fixture / Drain Alteration
companion_permits.reason: May be required if: Plumbing fixtures, drains, or water lines are added or moved.
companion_permits.required_if: Plumbing fixtures, drains, or water lines are added or moved
companion_permits.requirement_label: May be required based on scope

zoning_hoa_flag: For a downtown Los Angeles commercial office TI, HOA restrictions usually do not apply, but zoning, occupancy, and any historic district or overlay requirements can affect the project. If the work changes the exterior appearance, signage, or accessible route from the public way, additional zoning or Bureau of Engineering review may be triggered.
disclaimer: Data sourced Apr 28, 2026. Always verify current requirements directly with your local building department before starting work. Permit fees and requirements change frequently.
confidence_reason: Verified city/trade data found from official sources

_meta.job_type: 4,500 sf commercial office tenant improvement on 3rd floor: new demising partitions, office buildout, ceiling and lighting changes, sprinkler head relocation, electrical/data, accessible path of travel

code_section_source.title: [PDF] Title 51 PUBLIC HEALTH―SANITARY CODE Part XIV. Plumbing ...

inspection_process_source.title: [PDF] DEPARTMENT OF - Los Angeles City Planning

checklist: Verify your contractor license is current and valid in CA
Confirm your liability insurance is active (certificate may be required at permit office)
Have the property address and legal parcel number ready
Know the project value/cost — required on most permit applications

Identify altered primary-function area: if scope alters an area where major activity occurs, an accessible path of travel to the altered area is required unless technically infeasible / disproportionate (2010 ADA §202.4; 28 CFR §36.403(a))
20% disproportionality cap: ADA path-of-travel work exceeding 20% of alteration cost is disproportionate at the federal level, but lower-cost items must still be prioritized (28 CFR §36.403(f)(1))

Accessible exterior route: site arrival points + accessible parking/passenger loading + curb ramps + walking surfaces + ramps/landings + slope/cross-slope + door clearances to altered area (2010 ADA §§206.2.1, 206.4, 403, 404, 405, 406)

Restroom path + restroom upgrades: if restrooms serve the altered area, accessible route + door + turning space + lavatory + water closet + grab bars + clearances + signage (2010 ADA §§213, 603-606, 609, 703)
Parking + passenger loading: recalculate accessible-stall count + van space + access aisle + signage for affected parking serving the altered area (2010 ADA §§208, 502, 503; 28 CFR §36.403(e)(1))
Drinking fountains, phones, and signage along the path: tactile/visual signs, room ID, directional, fountain units, public phones if any (2010 ADA §§211, 216, 602, 703, 704)
Doors, thresholds, and hardware on the route: clear width + maneuvering clearance + opening force + thresholds + lever/operable hardware + protruding objects (2010 ADA §§303, 307, 309, 404)
ADA fixture counts per IBC Table 2902.1 (1 toilet + 1 lavatory per 1–25 occupants per sex; verify current edition)
Demising wall fire-rated assembly between tenants per IBC 706.3 (1-hour typical; 2-hour for higher-occupancy adjacencies)
Accessible-parking ratio per IBC 1106 + 1106.4 (1 per 25 spaces; 1 of every 6 must be van-accessible)
Exit access travel distance ≤200 ft (sprinklered) / ≤75 ft (non-sprinklered) per IBC 1017
Sprinkler head additions or relocations: hydraulic recalc + permit + 5-year cert update per NFPA 13/25
Lighting power density per IECC C405 (e.g. ≤0.79 W/sf for open-plan office)
Mechanical zoning per IMC 403 with tenant-controlled VAV / ductless on each occupied space
Fire alarm initiating-device coordination with base building (smoke detectors at HVAC return per NFPA 72 §17.7)
Accessible route from accessible entrance to all tenant areas per ADA §206 — no thresholds >½ in unbeveled
Means of egress: 2 exits required if occupant load >49 OR travel distance exceeds limits per IBC 1006
Energy code envelope + windows + insulation comply with IECC C402 if exterior wall is touched
Asbestos / lead survey for buildings pre-1980 if disturbing partitions, ceilings, or flooring
Glass + glazing: safety glazing per IBC 2406 in any pane within 24 in of doors or 60 in of floor

Confirm the required license is active and matches the application: Licensed California general contractor or the appropriate licensed trade contractor pulls the permit on the tenant improvement. For the electrical scope, a C-10 contractor pulls the electrical permit; for sprinkler work, the fire protection contractor pulls the sprinkler permit; the contractor's license number must appear on the application. The permit is still required even when a licensed contractor submits it.
Bring 2 copies of all documents to Los Angeles Department of Building and Safety (LADBS)

rejection_patterns.pattern: Missing CSLB license AND LA City Business Tax Registration Certificate
rejection_patterns.fix: Both required — get BTRC at finance.lacity.org before permit application

rejection_patterns.pattern: Bureau of Engineering approval missing for projects near public streets
rejection_patterns.fix: Submit to Bureau of Engineering separately from Building & Safety

fee_calculator.note: Provide job_value to calculate an exact fee where formulas are available.

expert_notes.title: California ADU 60-day ministerial shot clock
expert_notes.note: California state law (AB 881 / Govt Code 65852.2) requires the city to approve or deny ADU permits within 60 days of a complete application. If they exceed this, you have grounds to escalate.

expert_notes.title: California ADU impact-fee exemption under 750 sq ft
expert_notes.note: Impact fees waived for ADUs under 750 sq ft per California Government Code 65852.2(f)(3).

expert_notes.title: Title 24 / CF1R energy compliance
expert_notes.note: Title 24 energy compliance and CF1R documentation are required for new construction and alterations that change conditioned space. Solar mandate may apply for new detached ADUs depending on scope.
expert_notes.applies_to: New construction, ADUs, and conditioned-space alterations

expert_notes.title: Cal Fire VHFHSZ check
expert_notes.note: Check whether the parcel is in a Cal Fire Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone (VHFHSZ). Pasadena, Malibu, Santa Barbara, and parts of Los Angeles County can trigger additional fire-resistive material and defensible-space requirements.

expert_notes.title: CSLB licensing — $500 unlicensed-work threshold
expert_notes.note: California Contractors State License Board (CSLB) requires a license for any single project with combined labor + materials at $500 or more. Unlicensed contracting above that threshold is a misdemeanor (B&P §7028). Verify license + classification (e.g. B General Building, C-10 Electrical, C-36 Plumbing, C-20 HVAC) on the CSLB lookup before quoting; expired or suspended licenses void the contract and block lien rights.
expert_notes.applies_to: All paid construction work in California ≥ $500

expert_notes.title: SB 9 lot split + 2-unit ministerial path
expert_notes.note: SB 9 (Govt Code §65852.21 / §66411.7) lets owners of single-family-zoned parcels split a lot into two and/or build a duplex per resulting parcel under ministerial review (no public hearing, no CEQA). Combined with ADU/JADU rules this can yield up to 4 units on a former SFR lot. Owner must sign a 3-year owner-occupancy affidavit on lot splits. Cities can impose objective design standards but cannot deny qualifying SB 9 projects.
expert_notes.applies_to: Single-family-zoned residential infill and duplex conversions

expert_notes.title: California Building Standards Code triennial cycle (2025 → 2026 enforcement)
expert_notes.note: California adopts a new Title 24 / CBSC edition every three years. The 2025 California Building/Residential/Plumbing/Electrical/Mechanical/Energy/Green Building Standards Codes were published Jul 1, 2025 and become enforceable Jan 1, 2026 statewide. Permit applications submitted before Jan 1, 2026 may still be reviewed under the 2022 code at the AHJ's discretion — confirm which code edition applies before submitting drawings.
expert_notes.applies_to: Permit applications crossing the 2026-01-01 code-change boundary

expert_notes.title: CALGreen mandatory + EV-ready / solar-ready requirements
expert_notes.note: CALGreen (Title 24 Part 11) sets statewide green-building minimums: low-VOC finishes, water-use reduction (≤1.28 gpf toilets, ≤1.8 gpm kitchen faucets), EV-ready raceway in new SFR garages, and solar-ready zone for new low-rise residential. Tier 1/Tier 2 voluntary measures may be locally mandated (e.g., San Francisco, Santa Monica). Plan check rejection is common when the EV raceway or solar-ready zone is omitted from new SFR/ADU drawings.
expert_notes.applies_to: New construction and major remodels statewide

expert_notes.title: California municipal utility coordination
expert_notes.note: Local utility is Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) — service/interconnection coordination goes through the city utility, not SoCal Edison/PG&E.
expert_notes.applies_to: Electrical service, solar, battery, EV, and utility-interconnection work

expert_notes.title: Local VHFHSZ risk flag
expert_notes.note: Los Angeles may include Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone parcels. Verify the address on the Cal Fire VHFHSZ map before quoting exterior materials or ADU/solar scope.

hidden_triggers.title: Demising wall changes may require rated tenant separation and acoustic details
hidden_triggers.why_it_matters: Moving a tenant separation can affect fire-resistance continuity, penetrations, structure, ceiling plenum, egress, and landlord acoustic criteria. It is commonly under-scoped as 'just a partition.'
likely_required_actions: Show continuity to deck and protected penetrations

hidden_triggers.title: Sprinkler head relocation can require hydraulic recalculation and separate fire permit
hidden_triggers.why_it_matters: Ceiling, wall, storage, or occupancy changes can invalidate sprinkler spacing/coverage. Fire reviewers may require hydraulic calculations and current inspection/certification records.
likely_required_actions: Submit shop drawings/hydraulic calc if required

hidden_triggers.title: Commercial alteration can require accessible-route/path-of-travel upgrades
hidden_triggers.why_it_matters: Even when the requested work is not 'an ADA project,' altered primary-function areas can require accessible route, restroom, drinking fountain, parking, and entrance upgrades within the disproportionality cap.

hidden_triggers.title: Lighting changes trigger IECC lighting-power and automatic-control checks
hidden_triggers.why_it_matters: Replacing or reconfiguring lighting can require COMcheck/energy forms, lighting power density compliance, occupancy sensors, daylight controls, and automatic shutoff details.
likely_required_actions: Prepare lighting compliance / COMcheck if required
Show control zones and automatic shutoff

_fee_floor_components.scope_note: plan review + permit + sprinkler hydraulic recalc
_fee_floor_components.jurisdiction_rationale: LADBS + BOE/LAFD/Planning overlays; high cost-of-government metro [verify current LADBS fee estimator]
